author: stefanbratanov
content: Yeah, planning to do that, so TLDR, you are essentially having `BlindedExecutionPayloadEnvelope` where the payload has the roots for transactions and withdrawals right and storing blinded internally in databases, caches and when needed by RPC, API you reconstruct?

author: nflaig
content: planning to implement that later, we currently store the full `ExecutionPayloadEnvelope`, might be worth to define `BlindedExecutionPayloadEnvelope` somewhere in the spec, it's also required if we wanna make the vc<>bn stateful proposal flow more efficient, open discussion point here https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/580

author: potuz
content: I would rather if we keep the blinded stuff out of the spec, in fact I'm happy that Teku is doing something different than us

author: nflaig
content:
maybe not the CL spec but for beacon api we have to define it there

from comment here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/710831784991916072/1469662722802323488 it's seems like you are also in favor of introducing a blinded type
> The default should have no payload nor blob data being transferred either way, neither when the CL is acting as a proposer nor when the CL is acting as a builder (which CLs should implement by default)

so I think we can just define it on the beacon api and leave it out of the CL spec, currently that's where blinded types are defined anyways

author: potuz
content: Not there either: I'm pretty happy we have actual different objects for blinded envelopes. I don't see any reason why such an implementation specific object needs to be specified

author: nflaig
content: well on the api it needs to be the same, for db schema it doesn't matter

author: potuz
content: Ah you want to use this to avoid sending the whole thing to the VC? Yeah I'm conflicted about that

author: potuz
content: I would prefer the VC gets the whole Payload to sign and won't sign a blinded object

author: potuz
content: But I agree that if it's set in the beacon API for these purposes then it has to be standard

author: nflaig
content: not sure either, we definitley don't wanna pass the blobs around in the stateful flow, and same argument can be applied to the payload because the beacon node has it cached anyways

author: .paulharris
content: i guess it depends... may make sense to spec it if we only need blinded between CL peers, like for historic payloads wouldnt we currently need to fetch full payloads during backfill? and the EL would have the payload already because they do their own peer sync dont they? maybe blinded rpc would be a space saver...
